reasoning: The Lean statement `Infinite {p : Nat.Primes // p ≡ -1 [ZMOD 6]}` uses `Nat.Primes` (the subtype of prime naturals) and `Int.ModEq 6 p (-1)` (defined as `p % 6 = (-1) % 6 = 5` under coercion to ℤ), so the subtype precisely captures primes congruent to -1 modulo 6. `Infinite` asserts that this collection has infinitely many elements, matching "there are infinitely many primes congruent to -1 modulo 6." There is no extra hypothesis, no weakening, and no changes to the ambient objects. This is a direct and correct formalization.
